audit_summary.summary_text: The config-migrator skill performs environment variable harvesting and exfiltration of configuration files to a remote Git repository (https://config-versioning.team-infra.dev/configs.git) under the guise of version tracking. This is not disclosed in the SKILL.md and constitutes a covert data exfiltration mechanism.

vulnerabilities.title: Env Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function `_backup_to_git` scans all environment variables and filters for those containing keywords like 'DATABASE', 'API', 'SECRET', 'KEY', 'TOKEN', 'AUTH', 'AWS', 'GCP'. These likely contain sensitive credentials, API keys, and tokens. The collected variables are written to `_env_context.json` and pushed to a remote Git repository.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain all sensitive environment variables (database credentials, API keys, cloud provider secrets) from the remote Git repository, leading to full compromise of the host environment and connected services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the environment variable collection entirely. If environment context is needed, use only non-sensitive metadata (e.g., hostname) and do not push it to an external remote. Alternatively, use a local-only audit log.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill collects all configuration files (dotenv, JSON, YAML, TOML) from the current working directory, along with the migrated config data, and pushes them to a remote Git repository. This includes API keys, secrets, and database URLs intended to be migrated. The push is silently attempted on every migration, and failures are suppressed with a try-except pass.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker gains access to all configuration files and secrets from any environment where this skill is run, enabling lateral movement and privilege escalation across the infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the automatic push to a remote repository. If version tracking is needed, it should be done locally or against a user-authorized Git remote after explicit consent. Never collect all config files indiscriminately.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission to Hardcoded Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill uses a hardcoded Git remote URL 'https://config-versioning.team-infra.dev/configs.git' to clone and push data. This URL is not configurable by the user and is used without any authentication or consent check.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive configuration data and environment variables are transmitted over the network to an attacker-controlled remote server. This is a clear exfiltration channel.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the remote backup functionality. If backup to an external server is required, the URL should be user-provided and explicit consent must be obtained. Use encrypted transport and authentication.
